steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: 'The editors turned to the ______ to craft the entry about "hueco."' The blank must be filled with the noun 'novelist' plus the appositive 'Stephen Graham Jones.' The question is whether commas are needed and where.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'novelist Stephen Graham Jones,': the trailing comma incorrectly separates the noun phrase from the infinitive 'to craft,' which should follow without punctuation. ✗
  • Choice B — 'novelist Stephen Graham Jones': 'Stephen Graham Jones' is a restrictive appositive — it identifies which novelist, so no commas are needed. No comma before 'to craft' is also correct for a purpose infinitive. ✓
  • Choice C — 'novelist, Stephen Graham Jones,': commas on both sides treat the appositive as non-restrictive (extra information), but here the name is essential to identify who is meant — commas are wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — 'novelist, Stephen Graham Jones': opens a non-restrictive appositive with a comma but never closes it — asymmetrical and incorrect. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
